title: oxyceros curtisii
intro: Oxyceros curtisii, also known as the 'Strychnine Tree', is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Loganiaceae family. The plant contains alkaloids, particularly strychnine, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting medicinal properties such as analgesic, antipyretic, and anthelmintic effects. The health benefits of Oxyceros curtisii include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and intestinal parasites, as well as its application in skin conditions and as a natural insecticide. Herbal preparations of Oxyceros curtisii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties and potential side effects.
